definition_en: The highest law-making body of a country
example: A new bill was introduced in parliament yesterday.

definition_en: A system of government where citizens elect their leaders
example: A free press is essential in a democracy.

definition_en: Laws made by a government
example: New legislation on data privacy came into force last year.

definition_en: A course of action adopted by a government or organisation
example: The government announced a new housing policy.

definition_en: A formal vote to choose a person or government
example: The general election takes place in November.

definition_en: Dishonest or illegal behaviour by people in power
example: The investigation revealed widespread corruption in the ministry.

definition_en: A government formed by two or more political parties working together
example: The coalition government struggled to agree on key policies.

definition_en: A public vote on a single political question
example: The country held a referendum on independence.

definition_en: The set of fundamental principles by which a country is governed
example: Freedom of speech is guaranteed by the constitution.

definition_en: The management of relations between countries
example: Diplomacy is often more effective than military force.

definition_en: A penalty or measure applied to a country to force a change
example: Economic sanctions were imposed after the invasion.

definition_en: Political parties that are not in government
example: The opposition criticised the budget cuts.

definition_en: Government assistance provided to people in need
example: The welfare system supports those who are unemployed.

definition_en: The full right of a country to govern itself
example: The debate centred on national sovereignty.

definition_en: A system of government with many complicated rules and processes
example: The bureaucracy made it hard to get anything done quickly.

definition_en: An organised effort to achieve a political goal
example: She ran a successful campaign for mayor.

definition_en: A formal agreement between countries
example: The two countries signed a peace treaty.

definition_en: An official count of a population with various statistics
example: The national census is carried out every ten years.

definition_en: The rights of citizens to political and social freedom
example: The movement fought for equal civil rights for all people.

definition_en: The right to reject a decision or proposal
example: The president used his veto to block the law.
